diagnosis: Current Suspended Root Lens preview is technically stable but visually weak because it was generated from implementation primitives before reference constraints were translated into shape, material, and state rules.

decision.keep: central scroll object, four visual states, glass/root/archive subject
decision.reject: continuing to polish a procedural object without reference-driven silhouette and material targets
decision.next_move: Use references to choose a stronger object grammar before further Three.js or GLB work.

references.label: Scroll-driven 3D World
references.evidence_basis: Source describes a scroll-driven 3D environment built with Three.js, GSAP, WebGL, and Blender, where sections are composed scenes and scroll maps to camera movement and object animation.
references.shape_language: place-like scene composition, not isolated icon
references.state_language: single camera-take progression through composed scenes
references.borrow: Treat scroll states as cinematic scene beats and camera choreography.
references.avoid: Do not copy the portfolio identity, personal narrative, or specific scene models.
references.current_preview_gap: Our object changes state, but the surrounding scene still feels like a black demo canvas.
references.translation_command: Add one spatial context layer: shallow plinth, horizon membrane, or archive field that makes the object feel placed.

references.label: Weleda Open Garden
references.evidence_basis: Source frames the experience as an immersive online garden with 360 environments, WebGL rendering, AR botanicals, smooth transitions, and meditative mobile-first exploration.
references.shape_language: curated botanical environment with meaningful nature details
references.material_language: soft botanical tactility, natural specimen richness, meditative depth
references.state_language: exploration and discovery through connected garden moments
references.borrow: Make the botanical/root subject feel curated and alive rather than a technical placeholder.
references.avoid: Do not copy Weleda brand, plant assets, 360 environment, or soft wellness tone.
references.current_preview_gap: Our roots are abstract tubes; they do not yet feel like a selected specimen.
references.translation_command: Replace generic tubes with fewer, more deliberate root paths and endpoint buds with botanical logic.

references.label: iyO WebGL Exploded View
references.evidence_basis: Awwwards tags the element as WebGL, exploded view, scroll animation, 3D product exploration, and hardware interaction.
references.shape_language: central object with readable component hierarchy
references.material_language: product-grade surfaces, separation, and parts clarity
references.state_language: closed object to exploded or inspected object
references.borrow: Use exploded-view grammar for scan and archive states.
references.avoid: Do not turn the lens into generic hardware product advertising.
references.current_preview_gap: Our object has internal pieces but no clear hierarchy or intentional separation.
references.translation_command: Define 3 to 5 named internal layers: shell, membrane, core, roots, signals; animate them separately.

references.label: Spline AI Single Object Rule
references.evidence_basis: Spline AI docs recommend focusing on a single object, close-ups for detail, and objects that do not require high precision.
references.shape_language: one strong object, close-up first-frame, no full composition prompt
references.material_language: simple enough for generated geometry, material polish added later
references.borrow: Keep generation prompts object-only and use the web layer for scan/bloom/archive states.
references.avoid: Do not ask AI generation for the full page or complex cinematic scene.
references.current_preview_gap: The procedural preview is already too page-specific and not enough asset-specific.
references.translation_command: Before final code, generate or model the object as a clean asset with no page text or background.

references.label: False Earth
references.evidence_basis: Local DB records this as a WebGPU-world UI sculpture with curved horizon, grass field, cosmic beams, wave rings, lifecycle markers, and bounded procedural systems.
references.material_language: atmosphere, beams, dense procedural field, lifecycle glow
references.borrow: A surrounding field can make the object feel like part of a world rather than a floating diagram.
references.avoid: Do not make a full landscape or copy grass/flower motifs.
references.current_preview_gap: Our archive particles are too sparse and disconnected from the object.
references.translation_command: Turn archive particles into a bounded field emitted from root endpoints, not random dust.

references.label: WebGPU Scanning Depth Maps
references.evidence_basis: Local seed identifies this as a scan-depth and shader-material reference for technical reveal and inspection aesthetics.
references.material_language: scan plane, depth gradient, precise reveal pass
references.borrow: Make Root Scan a real volumetric inspection moment.
references.avoid: Do not use scan lines as decorative neon overlay.
references.current_preview_gap: Our scan plane is visible but does not reveal anything new.
references.translation_command: Bind scan progress to root opacity, membrane brightness, and core slice highlight.

references.label: Matrix Sentinels Trails
references.evidence_basis: Local seed frames this as a TSL particle-trail and dynamic motion structure reference.
references.material_language: particles as structured traces, not loose sparkle
references.borrow: Use particles as trace memory from the roots.
references.avoid: Do not add generic floating dots.
references.current_preview_gap: Archive particles currently read as background dust.
references.translation_command: Emit particle trails from specific amber buds and keep their arcs countable.

references.label: Codrops MeshLine Family
references.evidence_basis: Local DB records this as thickened vector routes, wire-grid scaffolding, shader ribbons, and sweep opacity states.
references.material_language: crisp route over wider translucent shader ribbon
references.state_language: subtle sweep offsets and route emphasis
references.borrow: Make root paths behave like authored route families, not random curves.
references.avoid: Do not drift into retro plotter/vector art.
references.current_preview_gap: Our root paths are curves but not a designed route system.
references.translation_command: Group roots into 2 to 3 visible route families with different width, opacity, and rhythm.

recommended_cards.label: Archive Instrument
recommended_cards.summary: Keep the glass lens, but make it more like a compact evidence instrument with separable shell, membrane, core, root, and signal layers.
recommended_cards.risk: Can become generic sci-fi product if too metallic.

recommended_cards.label: Living Specimen
recommended_cards.summary: Keep the botanical logic, but make roots fewer, more intentional, and curated like a preserved scientific specimen.
recommended_cards.risk: Can become cute terrarium or wellness garden.

recommended_cards.label: Contained World
recommended_cards.summary: Make the lens a small world cell with a shallow archive field or horizon membrane so the object feels placed, not floating on black.
recommended_cards.risk: Can grow into a full environment and lose the single-object constraint.

next_recommendation: Do not continue polishing the current geometry directly. Pick one recommended card, then rebuild the object grammar around that card.
